behavior_summary: When an actor (typically the Avatar) enters the item's fast trigger area, U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea marks the item active, sets its visual frame to an idle state, and spawns a persistent behavior process that repeatedly emits zap pulses.

The spawned coroutine (U8P_LITZAPNS::00BC) drives randomized visual frame changes, schedules timed waits via a FREE timer helper, plays a short animation/sound pulse (U8P_LITZAPNS::01C3), scans nearby tiles for overlapping items, filters found items to living NPCs, and invokes METHOD::0080 on each valid target (likely applying the zap effect).
Leaving the fast area runs U8P_LITZAPNS::leaveFastArea which attempts to clear process/timer resources (calls FREE::2A56) and reset item state/flags to stop ongoing activity.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea immediately sets item frame and spawns a long-running process (U8P_LITZAPNS::00BC) rather than performing all work inline.
U8P_LITZAPNS::00BC uses repeated spawn of FREE::1C19 as delay/timer primitives and yields/suspends between animation/effect steps, forming a periodic pulse loop.

Within each pulse, the coroutine randomizes frames/timing (urandom), runs a short sub-animation/sound routine (01C3), then scans area tiles and conditionally calls METHOD::0080 on overlapping living NPCs.
leaveFastArea invokes a cleanup/free routine (FREE::2A56) to cancel spawned timers/processes; process ids returned by spawn are stored/used for this cleanup.
open_questions: What exact game effect does METHOD::0080 apply to targeted NPCs (damage, knockback, status, or other)? — inferred but not explicit.
Are FREE::1C19 and FREE::2A56 generic timer/spawn management helpers (scheduling and cancel) or do they have additional side effects specific to this item?
What exact meaning do the different numeric frames convey (which frame indices correspond to idle, charging, firing visually)?
Are there any external conditions that stop the spawned coroutine other than leaveFastArea (e.g., global state changes or item destruction)?

main_function.name: U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea
main_function.purpose: Initialize the item's active state on fast-area entry, set its visible frame to idle, and spawn the main zap/effect process that will repeatedly produce zap pulses and affect nearby NPCs.
entry_conditions: Fast-area 'enter' event triggered when an actor enters the item's fast trigger region.
Called with the item instance as parameter (item pointer passed at BP+06h).

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry trigger: set item frame, mark/process-exclude item state, and spawn the persistent zap coroutine.

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPNS::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Area-exit cleanup: reset info/process-exclude and call FREE::2A56 to cancel/free timers or spawned processes associated with the item.

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPNS::00BC
function_map.role: Spawned persistent coroutine that sequences randomized animation, schedules delays, runs short animation/sound pulses, scans surrounding tiles for overlapping entities, filters to living NPCs, and invokes METHOD::0080 on each target.

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPNS::01C3
function_map.role: Short animation/sound pulse invoked by 00BC: plays sound, steps item through a few frames with delays, then restores idle frame.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Engine call to change the item's visible frame/animation state.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Timer/spawn helper used to create delays or scheduled callbacks (spawned repeatedly by 00BC and 01C3).

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Cleanup/free helper invoked on leaveFastArea to cancel timers/spawned resources linked to the item.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Random number generator used to vary frames, timing offsets, and compute parameters passed to METHOD::0080.

function_map.name: Item::overlaps
function_map.role: Test whether a scanned tile/address overlaps a candidate item (used to detect targets within effect area).

function_map.name: Item::isNpc
function_map.role: Check whether a found overlapping item is an NPC (non-NPCs are skipped).

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Check whether a found NPC target is dead; dead NPCs are skipped.

function_map.name: Npc::getDir
function_map.role: Return NPC facing/direction used with urandom to compute a parameter for METHOD::0080.

function_map.name: METHOD::0080
function_map.role: Invoked on valid NPC targets from 00BC with the item context and a computed parameter; likely applies the zap effect (damage/knockback/status) but exact semantics are inferred.

function_map.name: U8P_LITZAPNS::playSFX
function_map.role: Play sound effect for the zap/animation pulse (used in 01C3).

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the main persistent zap/effect coroutine so behavior continues after area entry.
Launch a short per-pulse animation/sound routine as part of each zap cycle.

external_calls.purpose: Set the item's visible frame to default/idle on area entry.
Cancel or free timers/processes associated with the item when leaving the area (cleanup).
Spawn short-lived timer processes to implement delays between animation/effect steps.
Generate random values to vary frame selection, timing, and compute arguments for METHOD::0080.
Detect items present at scanned tile addresses to compile candidate targets for the zap effect.
Filter overlapping items to NPCs only before further checks.
Skip dead NPCs when applying the zap effect.
Obtain NPC facing/direction to combine with randomness and compute a parameter passed to METHOD::0080.
Invoke the actual effect on a valid NPC target, passing the item context and a computed parameter (likely performs damage/knockback/status).
Play the zap pulse sound effect to accompany the visual animation.
Use timers to pace the short animation frame sequence.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When an actor (typically the Avatar) enters the item's fast trigger area, U8P_LITZAPNS::enterFastArea marks the item active, sets its visual frame to an idle state, and spawns a persistent behavior process that repeatedly emits zap pulses.
